data.narrative:
Astranis, a Series D space and aerospace company founded by John Gedmark and Ryan McLinko, carries a last post-money valuation of $3.5B following $350M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis relies on a comparables universe of eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers including ICEYE ($2.8B, Series E), Axiom Space ($2.5B, Series C), and Impulse Space ($1.8B, Series C), alongside five public aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 64.2x price-to-sales.

Two valuation methods are applicable given available data. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 1.5x multiplier from Series D to next-stage, implies $5.2B. Peer-median methodology across the eight private comparables—ranging from $1.0B to $2.8B with a median of $1.3B—implies $1.3B. Public comp anchoring cannot be applied due to lack of disclosed revenue. These two methods establish a triangulated range of $1.1B (low) to $6.0B (high) with a median of $3.3B.

The current $3.5B valuation sits near the median, reflecting positioning between peer-group medians and stage-progression expectations. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, which would unlock public comp validation via P/S multiples, and the wide dispersion across peer valuations ($1.0B–$2.8B), indicating material sensitivity to stage, market traction, and specific business model within the sector.
